patch.value: pm files --add <path> --note <text> (and pm docs) instead of requiring embedded path=...,note=... pair syntax. GitHub issue #170.

value.author: cli-fixes-agent
value.text: Design decisions (GH-170): (1) --note applies to EVERY link added by --add/--add-glob in the same invocation - one flag annotates the whole batch predictably for agents. (2) A per-entry embedded note= (pair syntax) wins over the standalone --note. (3) --note without any --add/--add-glob flag is a usage error (exit 2) with the missing flags surfaced in the recovery envelope; a glob that matches zero files is NOT an error (flag presence, not match count, gates the check). (4) Blank --note values are tolerated and leave entries unannotated. (5) MCP spelling is addNote on pm_files/pm_docs because the shared note parameter is the array-typed create/update note seed; server maps addNote->note (explicit options.note from pm_run wins). Implemented in shared linked-artifacts core (applyStandaloneNote) so files and docs behave identically.

patch.value: Linking a file/doc with a note requires the embedded pair syntax --add path=...,note=...; a bare --add has no way to attach a note.

pm files/pm docs accept a standalone --note <text> that annotates the whole --add/--add-glob batch (per-entry embedded note= takes precedence); MCP narrow tools expose it as addNote; usage error with recovery hints when --note is given without an add flag.

Shipped standalone --note for pm files/pm docs (GH-170): pm files <id> --add <path> --note <text> now attaches the note to every link added by --add/--add-glob in that invocation (embedded note= wins; --note without an add is a usage error). Wired across CLI flags, flag contracts, MCP pm_files/pm_docs (addNote param), bash/zsh completions, help examples, and docs; covered by new files-docs-command.spec.ts tests.
